title: Glasgow Prestwick Airport showcases cool chain investment to Indian Consul General

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Glasgow Prestwick Airport welcomed Indian Consul General Siddharth Malik to discuss UK-India trade and recent investment in enhanced cool chain infrastructure. The airport said Malik toured the cargo facilities with Kevin Braidwood, deputy chief executive of South Ayrshire Council, and business development officer Calum McPhail.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Talks focused on the UK-India Free Trade Agreement and Prestwick's upgraded facilities for Scottish salmon exporters, pharmaceuticals, life sciences and other perishable goods. The airport highlighted its in-house operating model, onsite bonded warehouse facilities and operational flexibility as part of its pitch to Indian businesses and Scottish exporters.

sections.heading: Next steps
sections.content: Nico Le Roux said the visit helped the airport explain trade opportunities and business culture in India before he attended the Air Cargo India exhibition in Mumbai from 25 to 27 February. There he planned meetings with carriers, freight forwarders and exporters on expanding UK-India cargo connectivity and other trade lanes from Prestwick.
